text.source_text: किमात्मकं पुनः शब्दमभिप्रेत्येदं शब्दप्रभवत्वमुच्यते ? स्फोटम् इत्याह । वर्ण पक्षे हि तेषामुत्पन्नप्रध्वंसित्वान्नित्येभ्यः शब्देभ्यो देवादिव्यक्तीनां प्रभव इत्यनुपपन्नं स्यात् । उत्पन्न प्रध्वंसिनश्च वर्णाः , प्रत्युच्चारणमन्यथा चान्यथा च प्रतीयमानत्वात् । तथा ह्यदृश्यमानोऽपि पुरुषविशेषोऽध्ययनध्वनिश्रवणादेव विशेषतो निर्धार्यते — ‘ देवदत्तोऽयमधीते , यज्ञदत्तोऽयमधीते ’ इति । न चा यं वर्णविषयोऽन्यथात्वप्रत्ययो मिथ्याज्ञानम् , बाधकप्रत्ययाभावात् । न च वर्णेभ्यो ऽर्थावगतिर्युक्ता । न ह्येकैको वर्णोऽर्थं प्रत्याययेत् , व्यभिचारात् । न च वर्णसमुदायप्रत्ययोऽस्ति , क्रमवत्वाद्वर्णानाम् । पूर्व पूर्ववर्णानुभवजनितसंस्कारसहितोऽन्त्यो वर्णोऽर्थं प्रत्याययिष्यतीति यद्युच्येत , तन्न । सम्बन्धग्रहणापेक्षो हि शब्दः स्वयं प्रतीयमानोऽर्थं प्रत्याययेत् , धूमादिवत् । न च पूर्वपूर्ववर्णानुभवजनितसंस्कारसहितस्यान्त्यवर्णस्य प्रतीतिरस्ति , अप्रत्यक्षत्वात्संस्काराणाम् । कार्य प्रत्यायितैः संस्कारैः सहितोऽन्त्यो वर्णोऽर्थं प्रत्याययिष्यतीति चेत् , न । संस्कारकार्यस्यापि स्मरणस्य क्रमवर्तित्वात् । तस्मा त्स्फोट एव शब्दः । स चै कैकवर्णप्रत्ययाहितसंस्कारबीजेऽन्त्यवर्णप्रत्ययजनितपरिपाके प्रत्ययिन्येकप्रत्ययविषयतया झटिति प्रत्यवभासते । न चा यमेकप्रत्ययो वर्णविषया स्मृतिः । वर्णानामनेकत्वादेकप्रत्ययविषयत्वानुपपत्तेः । तस्य च प्रत्युच्चारणं प्रत्यभिज्ञायमानत्वान्नित्यत्वम् , भेद प्रत्ययस्य वर्णविषयत्वात् । तस्मान्नित्याच्छब्दात्स्फोटरूपादभिधायकात्क्रियाकारकफललक्षणं जगदभिधेयभूतं प्रभवतीति ॥
text.transliteration_iast: kimātmakaṃ punaḥ śabdamabhipretyedaṃ śabdaprabhavatvamucyate ? sphoṭam ityāha | varṇa pakṣe hi teṣāmutpannapradhvaṃsitvānnityebhyaḥ śabdebhyo devādivyaktīnāṃ prabhava ityanupapannaṃ syāt | utpanna pradhvaṃsinaśca varṇāḥ , pratyuccāraṇamanyathā cānyathā ca pratīyamānatvāt | tathā hyadṛśyamāno'pi puruṣaviśeṣo'dhyayanadhvaniśravaṇādeva viśeṣato nirdhāryate — ‘ devadatto'yamadhīte , yajñadatto'yamadhīte ’ iti | na cā yaṃ varṇaviṣayo'nyathātvapratyayo mithyājñānam , bādhakapratyayābhāvāt | na ca varṇebhyo 'rthāvagatiryuktā | na hyekaiko varṇo'rthaṃ pratyāyayet , vyabhicārāt | na ca varṇasamudāyapratyayo'sti , kramavatvādvarṇānām | pūrva pūrvavarṇānubhavajanitasaṃskārasahito'ntyo varṇo'rthaṃ pratyāyayiṣyatīti yadyucyeta , tanna | sambandhagrahaṇāpekṣo hi śabdaḥ svayaṃ pratīyamāno'rthaṃ pratyāyayet , dhūmādivat | na ca pūrvapūrvavarṇānubhavajanitasaṃskārasahitasyāntyavarṇasya pratītirasti , apratyakṣatvātsaṃskārāṇām | kārya pratyāyitaiḥ saṃskāraiḥ sahito'ntyo varṇo'rthaṃ pratyāyayiṣyatīti cet , na | saṃskārakāryasyāpi smaraṇasya kramavartitvāt | tasmā tsphoṭa eva śabdaḥ | sa cai kaikavarṇapratyayāhitasaṃskārabīje'ntyavarṇapratyayajanitaparipāke pratyayinyekapratyayaviṣayatayā jhaṭiti pratyavabhāsate | na cā yamekapratyayo varṇaviṣayā smṛtiḥ | varṇānāmanekatvādekapratyayaviṣayatvānupapatteḥ | tasya ca pratyuccāraṇaṃ pratyabhijñāyamānatvānnityatvam , bheda pratyayasya varṇaviṣayatvāt | tasmānnityācchabdātsphoṭarūpādabhidhāyakātkriyākārakaphalalakṣaṇaṃ jagadabhidheyabhūtaṃ prabhavatīti ||

translation.literal_translation: What kind of 'word' is intended when saying the world arises from word? The answer: sphota. If one takes perishable phonemes as word, emergence of divine individuals from eternal words would not be coherent, since phonemes are produced and destroyed and differ across each utterance. We distinguish speakers merely by hearing recitation sounds, and this difference-cognition is not false absent a defeater. Nor can meaning be established from isolated phonemes, due to irregularity. Nor is there cognition of the whole phoneme-cluster simultaneously, since phonemes occur sequentially. If one says the final phoneme, aided by traces of previous ones, conveys meaning, that fails: a sign conveys only when apprehended with relation known; but traces are not directly perceived. If one invokes memory as trace-effect, memory too is sequential. Therefore word is sphota: when trace-seeds from successive phoneme-cognitions ripen with the final phoneme-cognition, a single cognition flashes forth. This single cognition is not mere phoneme-memory, since many phonemes cannot be the object of one cognition as such. Its repeat-recognition across utterances indicates permanence; difference-cognition belongs to phonemes, not to sphota. Therefore from eternal denotative word in sphota-form, the world characterized by action, agent, and result arises as denotatum.
translation.technical_translation: Presents sphota-vada defense: rejects varna as ultimate semantic unit due to sequentiality and perishability; posits instantaneous unitary sphota-cognition as stable denotative basis for veda-eternity and shabda-prabhava.
translation.interpretive_note: Metaphysics of language is used to protect Vedic eternality under production-talk.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

A later philological pass should revisit technical choices around sphota/varna vocabulary in records 1.3.068 and 1.3.069.
